kept.text:
$META might have just positioned itself as the biggest robotics play of the next decade.

Meta acquired Assured Robot Intelligence, a startup developing artificial intelligence models for robots, as part of a major initiative to build humanoid technology.

If this plays out…

Meta becomes the operating system for robots.

BULLISH

profile_bio.description: I like to talk about stocks, real estate, AI, Bitcoin, and financial freedom. Let’s escape the matrix. Not financial advice.

quoted_tweet.text:
Excited to share that Assured Robot Intelligence (ARI) has joined @Meta to help build the future of humanoid intelligence!

When we started ARI one year ago, our mission was clear: achieve physical AGI. Through deep customer engagements and real-world deployments, it became clear to us that serving the massive opportunity ahead requires training a truly general-purpose physical agent.

We believe this agent will be humanoid — and that scaling will come from learning directly from human experience, not teleoperation alone. Meta’s ecosystem brings together the key components needed to make this vision possible. We will be joining Meta Superintelligence Labs (MSL) to help bring personal superintelligence into the physical world.

We are incredibly grateful to the brilliant minds, robotics researchers, engineers, partners, and supporters who have worked with us on this journey. Thank you to our investors and angels, led by @aixventureshq , for believing in our mission.

This is just the beginning.

profile_bio.description:
Co-founder of Assured Robot Intelligence (ARI)
Associate Professor @UCSDJacobs
Postdoc @berkeley_ai

kept.text:
$Meta içinde Claudeonomics diye bir liderlik tablosu varmış. Yapay zekayı en çok kullanan çalışanları sıralıyormuş. 
85 binden fazla çalışan takip edilmiş, 30 günde 60 trilyon token tüketilmiş.

Mühendis başına yıllık AI harcaması 100-300 bin $ arasındaymış.
Yani neredeyse bir mühendisin yıllık maaşına denk. Tablonun altındaki çalışanlar ise işten çıkarılma riski taşıyormuş :)
(Az kullanan çalışanları çıkarmak için zemin hazırlanıyor gibi geldi bana)

$Meta önce rakiplerinin araçlarını kullanıp bilgi sızdırmamak için kendi aracı Devmate i geliştirmiş, çalışanlar beğenmemiş yetersiz bulmuşlar. 
En son kuralı gevşetmişler, müşteri verisine dokunmadığın sürece rakiplerin aracı Claude Code’u kullanabilirsin demişler.

Yapay zeka sayesinde verimlilik şirket için 100 kat artmış ama gelire etkisi 1 kat bile değil. 

Üstüne 100 milyar $ bandında capex yapan sektörün en iyi AI uzmanlarına milyarlarca dolar bonus dağıtıp ekiplerine katmaya çalışan bir şirket $META, ilginç bir durum.

kept.text:
A common misconception when you buy a LEAPS call:

“The price needs to be above the breakeven price for you to make money”

That is absolutely NOT true

Example: 

I bought this $META 1/21/28 $800 call for $61.75 on March 26

The breakeven price is $861.75

But…

That only matters at expiration date

I sold this call for $88.25 on April 9 while $META was barely at $630

So yeah…

Even though the price is nowhere close to $861.75…

I was able to profit almost $2,700 per contract in less than a month

🔥💰

tweet.text:
$IREN Sweetwater 1.4GW site energized as scheduled.

A 1.4GW site, fully online, at a time when nearly half of all planned U.S. data centers are either delayed or cancelled altogether.

And the timing couldn't be better. This week, $GOOGL, $AMZN, $MSFT, and $META all said the same thing on their earnings calls that they can't get enough compute. Every single one is raising CapEx and that's before you factor in the insatiable demand from OpenAI, Anthropic, and the rest of the AI arms race.

We are still early.

tweet.text:
$AVGO $NVDA  $GOOG $META  $AAPL 

Broadcom 這季真正詭異的不是營收，是結構。
AI 在狂飆，其他業務直接原地踏步。這不是全面復甦，這是單一引擎硬拉整台公司往前衝。

但市場還在那邊問貴不貴，看 P/E？方向錯了。
重點是——這家公司已經不是在「賣晶片」，而是在卡住 AI 的入口。
六個最大 AI 玩家，全都綁在同一條供應鏈上。產能鎖到 2028，封裝排好，記憶體先買。
這不是預測，是訂單已經簽完，工廠已經在跑。

更狠的是，客戶不是買通用 GPU，而是直接客製晶片。
代表什麼？代表未來的算力，不是誰貨多誰贏，是誰被綁在裡面誰付錢。
所以這局很簡單：
別再問它貴不貴。
要問的是——當 AI 變成基礎設施，誰在收過路費？

tweet.text:
Big Tech CapEx has reached unprecedented levels:

The combined CapEx of Amazon, $AMZN, Google, $GOOG, Meta, $META, and Microsoft, $MSFT, is expected to surge +98% YoY, to a record $715 billion in 2026.

This is nearly 3 TIMES the amount spent in 2024 and more than 5 TIMES 2023 levels.

Amazon now expects CapEx to approach a record $200 billion this year.

This is followed by Google and Microsoft which are both guiding $190 billion in CapEx for 2026.

Meta has also raised its expectations by +$10 billion to $125-$145 billion.

To put this differently, each company is projected to spend nearly as much in 2026 as they did in the previous 2 years combined, or more.

The AI Revolution is set to accelerate.

quoted_tweet.text:
Good morning ☕️🦀🇺🇸 Happy Saturday!

The stock market had a strong week overall for the period ending Friday, May 2, 2026 (trading days April 28–May 1), with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq posting weekly gains and new record closes, while the Dow was slightly negative.

Major Indices Performance (Week of April 28–May 1)
•S&P 500: Closed at 7,230.12 (+0.29% on Friday; weekly gain ~0.3–0.6% range across reports). It hit new all-time highs multiple times.
•Nasdaq Composite: Closed at 25,114.44 (+0.89% on Friday; stronger weekly gain). Also set new records, driven by tech.
•Dow Jones Industrial Average: Closed at 49,499.27 (-0.31% on Friday; slight weekly loss).

April as a whole was exceptional: The S&P 500 gained over 9–10% (best monthly performance since 2020), Nasdaq ~15% (best since 2020), and Dow ~7%. This reflected a sharp recovery from earlier geopolitical pressures.

Key Drivers and Themes
•Strong Corporate Earnings: A heavy earnings week boosted sentiment. Big Tech results were mixed but generally supportive:
◦Apple (AAPL) reported strong Q2 results on April 30: Revenue $111.2B (+17% YoY), EPS $2.01 (+22% YoY), beating estimates. Shares rose >3% on Friday.
◦Other highlights included Alphabet (strong results), Caterpillar (big Dow boost), and mixed Big Tech reactions around AI capex/spending concerns (e.g., Meta, Microsoft).
•Geopolitics and Oil: Ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions and Strait of Hormuz disruptions kept oil volatile and elevated (WTI near $100+/barrel at points, with spikes higher). This pressured some sectors but markets largely shrugged it off amid ceasefire hopes and resilient earnings.
•Breadth and Style: Tech and growth led (especially AI-related), while broader market participation was narrower. Value and small-caps were more mixed.
•Economic Backdrop: Resilient U.S. data (e.g., retail sales, PMIs) supported the rally despite Fed signals and inflation concerns. Markets showed optimism heading into May.

Sector and Market Notes
•Tech, communication services, and select cyclicals (e.g., industrials) outperformed.
•Energy was volatile with oil prices.
•Defensive areas lagged somewhat.
•International markets were mixed, with some developed markets underperforming U.S. indices.

Overall Sentiment: Bullish recovery mode. The market demonstrated resilience amid geopolitical noise, rewarding earnings strength and AI optimism. “Sell in May” concerns were discussed but not dominating, given recent momentum.

This recap is based on closing levels and reports as of May 1–2, 2026. Markets move fast—always check real-time sources for the latest.
